commands.review_summary:
The PR adds structured Plugin SDK thread-route inputs, migrates bundled channel route builders, updates Telegram topic routing, and documents the new helper contract.

PR surface: Source +125, Tests +103, Docs +4. Total +232 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an SDK/channel-routing refactor rather than a bug report with a current-main failure path. The changed behavior is source-reviewable and test-described, but live delivery proof is still missing.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer approval for the public SDK contract and real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for a repair lane.

target.title: refactor(plugin-sdk): split session and route thread ids
target.body:
## Summary

- Add explicit `replyToThread` / `thread` SDK inputs with separate `sessionThreadId` and `routeThreadId` values.
- Deprecate legacy `replyToId` / `threadId` inputs while keeping compatibility, and migrate bundled channel plugins to the new API.
- Update Telegram routing so persisted session identity can include chat/topic scope while the outbound route keeps the native topic id.
- Document the new SDK shape and recovered-thread mapping guidance without introducing provider-specific delivery vocabulary into core.

## Why

`buildThreadAwareOutboundSessionRoute(...)` previously had one thread id doing two jobs: session persistence and outbound route metadata. That worked for most plugins, but Telegram forum topics need a scoped session identity such as `chatId:topicId` while the outbound route still needs the native topic id. Splitting those concepts makes the mismatch explicit and keeps the helper generic: core owns route/session semantics, plugins own how provider-native ids map into them.

## Compatibility

- Existing plugin callers using `replyToId` / `threadId` continue to work.
- The old fields are marked deprecated in favor of `replyToThread` / `thread`.
- The temporary `deliveryThreadId` naming never lands in the PR branch; the public additive shape is `routeThreadId`.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-sdk/core.ts src/plugin-sdk/thread-aware-outbound-session-route.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-session-route.ts extensions/slack/src/channel.ts extensions/qa-channel/src/channel.ts extensions/clickclack/src/channel.ts extensions/matrix/src/session-route.ts extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.ts docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/thread-aware-outbound-session-route.test.ts` (14 tests)
- Testbox `tbx_01ksyep04yhwk7584aamkanqnr`: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/thread-aware-outbound-session-route.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/session-route.test.ts` (14 SDK tests, 5 Telegram tests)
- Autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: thread-aware outbound routing now represents persisted session thread identity separately from outbound route thread metadata.
Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox on Linux via OpenClaw CI workflow.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/thread-aware-outbound-session-route.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/session-route.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: Testbox `tbx_01ksyep04yhwk7584aamkanqnr` passed targeted SDK and Telegram tests.
Observed result after fix: SDK helper preserves deprecated alias behavior, keeps explicit session/route ids separate, normalizes explicit string route ids, and keeps Telegram topic route ids numeric.
What was not tested: live provider delivery against Discord, Slack, Matrix, Mattermost, QA channel, ClickClack, or Telegram.
target.author: steipete
